findingDetails.claim: Live prompt-improver agent metadata can preserve or emit the removed /prompt command/path instead of /prompt-improve.

findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: An undocumented /prompt alias could exist outside the markdown command tree, but the approved spec explicitly selected a rename rather than alias retention.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if a current command router intentionally aliases /prompt to /prompt-improve and documents that compatibility for agent handoff inputs.

findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to an advisory note if .opencode/skills/prompt-models is an intentional compatibility link.

findingDetails.claim: The prompt-improve command permits a custom save path without a documented containment or overwrite guard.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Searched the command, sk-prompt skill tree, and 124 spec docs for custom-path, workspace/outside, overwrite, and save-location guard language; also checked read-only prompt-improver agent boundaries and hub resource guards.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The executor runtime may independently sandbox writes, and the user must explicitly choose Q2=C, so this is not proven arbitrary filesystem compromise. The command contract itself still lacks the containment rule downstream executors need.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if the command runtime enforces workspace/spec-folder write containment and no-overwrite semantics before command markdown steps are executed.
findingDetails.title: Custom save path lacks containment and overwrite guard
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact searches for custom-path, workspace/outside, overwrite, and save-location guard language found custom-path write steps but no containment/overwrite guard in the command; skill-local resource loaders contain guard patterns.

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Non-trivial command/agent/skill review scope with mutating command surface and cross-runtime agent mirrors.

targetSelection.selectionReason: Security trust-boundary slice: mutating command save path, read-only agent boundary, hub resource guards, and read-only prompt-models packet surface.
targetSelection.graphStatus: stale: git HEAD changed and 156 stale files exceed selective threshold; graph not used for proof
targetSelection.semanticSearchStatus: not_used: exact grep/read coverage was sufficient

searchLedger.hypothesis: The prompt save command may permit path escape or overwrite through the custom path branch.

searchActions.result: Q2=C creates `[custom-path]/` and writes `[custom-path]/[filename].md` without documented safe-root or no-overwrite guard.
Found custom path write and validation language, but no containment or overwrite guard in the command.

searchLedger.rationale: The command has a mutating tool surface and lacks a documented safe-root/overwrite rule for custom save targets.

searchLedger.invariant: Prompt-improver agents must not execute the enhanced prompt or invoke tools/agents/CLIs/MCP as a side effect.

searchActions.result: OpenCode agent denies write/edit/bash/task and both runtime agent bodies state they never invoke external CLIs, MCP tools, commands, skills, or agents.

searchLedger.rationale: The live agent contracts keep prompt content as output-only and forbid execution/mutation in this slice.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Verified by direct reads of both runtime agent definitions.

searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Verified skill-root relative path guards by direct read.

searchLedger.invariant: prompt-models remains read-only and must not gain workspace mutation or executor mechanics.

searchActions.result: mode-registry forbids Write/Edit/Task/Bash for prompt-models and marks mutatesWorkspace false; prompt-models rules forbid runtime logic and shell commands.

searchLedger.rationale: The prompt-models packet remains a read-only profile guidance surface in this slice.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Verified by direct read of registry and packet rules.

findingDetails.claim: The /prompt-improve Q2=B branch creates specs/[NNN]-[topic]/prompts/ from prompt topic text without a documented slug, containment, or shell-argument guard.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Searched the command and spec packet for slug/sanitize/containment wording around topic, new spec folder, filename, custom path, and save path; found only filename hyphen-case guidance and the previously recorded custom-path containment finding for Q2=C, not a guard for Q2=B.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The runtime operator may manually normalize [topic] before creating the folder, but the command contract does not require that normalization and is intended to be executable from the workflow text.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade or resolve if the command explicitly derives a safe slug for [topic], rejects path separators/shell metacharacters, resolves under the intended specs root, and refuses overwrite/collision cases before directory creation.

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: multi-file spec/skill/command/agent review with mutating command path handling and prior active findings

searchActions.result: Q0 accepts topic text and Q2=B derives save_path through specs/[NNN]-[topic]/.
Q2=B creates specs/[NNN]-[topic]/prompts/ with mkdir -p; only filename generation has hyphen-case wording.
No topic slug/containment guard found; prior Q2=C custom-path issue is separate.

searchLedger.rationale: The branch presents a mutating command contract that uses topic text as a path segment without requiring safe derivation.
